We further recommended to the GSO to regularly update and submit the PPE cards for easy monitoring of the account.
observations.managementAction: All municipal properties including Infrastructure Assets were discussed in detail in Part II of this Report and the GSO.
observations.reason: No reconciliation conducted between the records of the Accounting and the RPCPPE.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive instruct the Municipal Accountant and such other LGU official concerned to immediately submit the Feasibility Study for the loan financing arrangement. We further recommended provide adequate disclosure in the Notes to Financial Statement of the credit financing entered by Municipality in the preparation of the financial statements for the ensuing year to ensure transparency of its transactions.
observations.managementAction: The Integrated Public Market Terminal was already reflected in the 2019 FS. The feasibility study was already submitted.
observations.reason: Feasibility Study was already submitted. While the financing agreement was reflected in the 2019 FS.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive instruct the Municipal Treasurer and Municipal Accountant to submit the disbursement vouchers together with their required supporting documents as well as the cancelled checks and direct the person(s) involved to strictly adhere to the timely compliance of submission of the required documents.
observations.managementAction: Disbursement vouchers together with their required supporting documents were submitted. Some DVs amounting to P808,462.59 were still not submitted by the Municipal Treasurer and Municipal Accountant.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive instruct the Municipal Treasurer and Municipal Accountant to submit the disbursement vouchers together with their required supporting documents as well as the cancelled checks and direct the person(s) involved to strictly adhere to the timely compliance of submission of the required documents.
observations.managementAction: Some disbursement vouchers are still not submitted and were issued with a Notice of Suspension.
observations.reason: Some DVs are still not submitted and were issued with a Notice of Suspension.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive see to it that the projects/programs under the LPRAP are workable and realistic to avoid problems during implementation. It is further recommended that the LCE employ measures to expedite the process of the implementation of the projects received from the national government to ensure that the fund will be utilized, and its purpose will be realized. Otherwise, immediately return to the source agency the unutilized amount in adherence to COA Circular No. 94-013.
observations.managementAction: The management is still waiting for some proposals and revised program of work on which the proposed projects need to be approved by the source agency.
observations.reason: Some proposals still need revisions and some are waiting for approval by the source agency.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the LGU review the hiring procedure and management of Job Order/Casual personnel and require the immediate submission of the Accomplishment Reports and Daily Time Records for all payments made to JO/Casual personnel in accordance with COA Circular No. 2012-001.
observations.managementAction: The number of JO/Casuals was reduced although there is no established procedure yet for the hiring and management of these personnel.
observations.reason: The previous HRMO did not turn over complete records and documents; no structured procedure yet.

observations.recommendation: We recommended to the Solid Waste Management Board headed by the Local Chief Executive to fast tract the establishment of its sanitary landfill and enforced the operation of its MRF. We further recommended to make ordinances in relation to solid waste management fees to support its operation and achieve the objectives/purpose of the Ecological Solid Waste Management Program.
observations.managementAction: The Solid Waste Management Board headed by the Local Chief Executive fast tracked the establishment of its sanitary landfill and enforced the operation of its MRF.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the Municipal Accountant to prepare bank reconciliation statement accurately and on a monthly basis for all the bank accounts of the agency. Further, a revised bank reconciliation statement should be prepared for the General Fund to reflect valid and actual outstanding checks to be deducted from the balance of the account per bank statement and come up with an accurate balance of the account.
observations.managementAction: The Local Accountant submitted a revised bank reconciliation statement and corrected the bank errors.
observations.reason: Revisions to the bank reconciliation still need to be implemented; some checks were treated as outstanding though cancelled.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the LCE require the recipient agencies to submit a Report on the Status of the Project as well as Accomplishment/Liquidation Report validated by the Municipal Engineer or his authorized representative. The status report should be submitted to COA for evaluation. We further recommended strict observation of Section 53.9 and Section 8, Annex H of the Revised IRR, RA 9184 with complete supporting documents before payments are made.
observations.managementAction: The implementation of these projects is still on-going; Report on Status of Implementation of these projects as well as inspection reports and certificate of completion were not yet submitted to COA.
observations.reason: Some implementing agencies have not submitted Accomplishment/Liquidation Reports and inspection reports; procurement lapses noted.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the Municipal
Accountant to prepare a correct bank reconciliation statement to reflect all
reconciling items that require adjustments and correction in the book of
accounts, and henceforth prepare and submit bank reconciliation statement
regularly so that errors could be detected and corrected immediately.

We also recommended to direct the Municipal Treasurer to determine the
existence of stale checks considered as outstanding by sending the payee a written
notice of the existence of the checks (Section 59, Vol. I, NGAS Manual for LGUS).

We recommended to the Management to conduct a complete physical inventory
count of all municipal properties including Infrastructure Assets owned by the
Municipality and submit a complete RPCPPE and reconcile it with the balance
of the Property, Plant and Equipment account to permit a fair presentation of the
properties owned by the municipality. We further recommend to the GSO to
regularly update and submit the PPE cards for easy monitoring of the account.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive instruct the Municipal
Accountant and such other LGU official concerned to immediately submit the
Feasibility Study for the loan arrangement. We further recommended to the
Municipal Accountant to provide adequate disclosure in the Notes to Financial
Statement of the Credit Financing entered by Municipality in the preparation of
the financial statements for the ensuing year to ensure transparency of its
government transactions.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive to direct the officials
concerned to refrain from granting cash advances other than for official travel to
personnel who are not properly bonded and dully appointed as disbursing officers.
We further recommended to submit immediately the liquidation reports for the said
cash advances to determine the propriety, legality and reliability of the transaction.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive instruct the Municipal
Treasurer and Municipal Accountant to immediately submit the disbursement
vouchers together with their required supporting documents as well as the
cancelled checks and direct the person(s) involved to strictly adhere to the timely
compliance of submission of the required documents.

This is without prejudice to the issuance of the Notice of Suspension and/or Notice
of Disallowance, if warranted.

We recommend that the Local Chief Executive direct the Municipal Accountant to prepare bank reconciliation statement accurately and correctly and on a monthly basis for all the bank accounts of the agency. Further, a corrected or revised bank reconciliation statement should be prepared for the General Fund to reflect valid and actual outstanding checks to be deducted from the balance of the account per bank statement and come up with an accurate balance of the account.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant submitted a revised bank reconciliation statement.
observations.reason: Checks issued since 2016 and beyond were still reported outstanding on the bank accounts of the agency.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the LCE direct the procurement of ordinary and common-use supplies and equipment only at the Procurement Service (PS) Depot; in cases when these supplies are unavailable at PS Depot, procure from the nearest supplier with proof that the same are unavailable at the PS Depot.
observations.managementAction: The management has procured some ordinary and common-use supplies from PS Depot; however in cases when these supplies are unavailable at PS, procurement is done with the nearest supplier.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive instruct the Municipal Treasurer and Municipal Accountant to immediately submit the disbursement vouchers together with the required supporting documents as well as the cancelled checks and direct the person(s) involved to strictly adhere to the timely compliance of submission of the required documents.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Treasurer and Municipal Accountant to submit the disbursement vouchers together with the required supporting documents as well as the cancelled checks.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the LGU exert extra effort in the implementation of the projects and make necessary arrangements, if necessary in order to fully comply with the Memoranda of Agreement, and other requirements of the project proposals which still needs to be approved by the concerned agencies.
observations.managementAction: Majority of these projects have already been implemented. For few others which have extra proposals remain unimplemented as of year-end, some needs to be approved by the source agency.
observations.reason: Revisions to the POW and program proposals which still need to be approved by the source agency.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the LCE require the concerned recipient agencies to submit a Report on the Status of the Project Implementation as well as inspection reports and certificate of completion validated by the Municipal Engineer or his authorized representative.
observations.managementAction: The concerned recipient agencies to submit a Report on the Status of the Project Implementation as well as inspection reports and certificate of completion validated by the Municipal Engineer or his authorized representative.
observations.reason: The Status Report of the Project Implementation was not yet submitted to COA.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the LCE direct the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer to remit regularly the Special Education Tax collected to the SEF account and SEF disbursements should only be made under the SEF account.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer remitted the Special Education Tax and ensured SEF disbursements are made under the SEF account.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the SWMPB formalize a change management strategy in the implementation of a realistic and doable Solid Waste Management Plan with the application of the SMART strategy, develop an action/implementation Plan, draw a robust governance arrangement for the Plan and identify possible risks in a comprehensive manner for SWMB to facilitate proper implementation, monitoring and measurement of results, and to establish accountability for results.
observations.managementAction: SWMB to formalize and implement a realistic Solid Waste Management Plan with strategies and action plans.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management require the concerned officials and employees to liquidate immediately their outstanding cash advances, and there should be no additional cash advance given to any employee unless the previous cash advance given to him is first settled or a proper accounting thereof is made. Moreover, require the Accountant to reclassify the undeposited collections which were included as cash advances to an appropriate account.
observations.managementAction: The concerned officials and employees to liquidate their outstanding cash advances and the Accountant to reclassify undeposited collections to proper account titles.

observations.recommendation:
We recommend that the Local Chief Executive direct the Municipal
Accountant to prepare bank reconciliation statement accurately and on a monthly
basis for all the bank accounts of the agency. Further, a corrected or revised bank
reconciliation statement should be prepared for the General Fund to reflect valid
and actual outstanding checks to be deducted from the balance of the account per
bank statement and come up with an accurate balance of the account.

We further recommend that the LCE direct the Municipal Treasurer and
Municipal Accountant to make representation with LBP - Borongan Branch to make
the necessary adjustments to the error of debiting the LGU’s account for a returned
check of P14,250.00, and direct the Municipal Treasurer to determine the existence
of stale checks considered as outstanding checks by sending a written notice to the
payees (Section 59, Vol. I, NGAS Manual for LGUs)

We recommend that the LCE direct the procurement of ordinary or common-
use supplies and equipment only at the Procurement Service (PS) Depot. In instances
when supplies to be procured are not available at the PS, secure proof that the same
are not available thereat before the decision to procure the items from other supplier
is made as provided for under Section 52 of the Revised IRR, RA 9184.

We recommend the Local Chief Executive direct the Municipal Treasurer
and Municipal Accountant to immediately submit the disbursement vouchers
together with the required supporting documents and direct the person(s) involved
to strictly adhere to the timely compliance of submission of the required documents.

We recommend that: a. An R operational Should be prepared by the Senior Citizen proponent; b. Ensure that on the MSWDO and LGU is guided with the MOA on the Roles and Responsibilities of the Parties in the implementation of the project, this year; c. Submit to this Commission a copy of the Status Report of the Projects not later than December 15,2016; d. Moving forward, for future projects, ensure that these are properly complied with in accordance with the MOA, any deviation which is beyond the control of the LGU should be properly reported to the concerned authorities/stakeholder.
observations.managementAction: AA A copy of operational Plan 201 6- 001 was provided this office. Botika ni Lolo/Lola in the implementation of the project, this year, with proper concurrence of all LPRAT member and approved by the LCE. Please see submitted communication from the DTI,DSWD, LCE,senior Citizen and MSWDO.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the RHU Department require the Department to: a. Immediately craft a well-defined guidelines through an internal policy, on the Request for Procurement, Receipt, Disposition and Monitoring of the Medicines and Medical supplies/equipment to ensure that accountabilities and safeguarding, including risk ownership are in place while in its custody. b. The written guidelines may include, among others: b.1.Request for Medicines: 1. Barangay Health Profiling should be done annually to all 42 barangays; 2. This in turn should be submitted to the Public Health Nurse (PHN) for the preparation of the LGU Consolidated Report on Barangay Health Profile; 3. The Consolidated Report on Barangay Health Profile should be reconciled with the Medicines and Medical Supplies/equipment Inventory Report 4. The result of the reconciliation will now serve as the basis for the preparation for the request of stocks to avoid shortage of medicines/medical supplies/equipment. 5. A Purchase Request should be prepared by the MHO and forwarded to the LCE for the approval and eventual procurement. b.2. Receipt of Medicines: 1. All receipt of medicines and medical supplies/equipment should be thoroughly inspected by the Custodian and an RHU representative simultaneously using the Delivery Receipt and Purchase Request as their basis. Take note of the Quantity of Medicines and the Expiration dates; 2. The Medicines received should be posted in the logbook inventory. A separate set of logbook should be utilized for stocks from different sources (LGU/ DOH/Province) 3. Ensure that these are properly stocked 4. In cases where the custodian is not available, there should be a medicine custodian-alternate, designated by the MHO, and a proper turn-over should be made. 5. There should be a registry for medicines for all receipts and issuances to ensure proper tracking of the medicines and /medical supplies/equipment b.3. Issuances of Medicines 1. A copy of the medical receipts/request for release from the RHU/Mayor should be evidenced every time there is an issuance of medicines and medical supplies/equipment 2. It should be attached to the Authorized RHU Medicine slip and signed by the custodian and recipient 3. It should be filed in a designated folder for Medicines Issue Slip for easy reference. 4. The recipient should sign in the logbook upon release of the medicines 5. A monthly Report of issuances should be prepared and these should be reconciled with the stocks available b.4. Monitoring: 1. Conduct of Physical Inventory/ Inspection Report at least twice a month 2. Post the List of Inventory In a conspicuous place of the RHU c. The MHO, should strictly ensure the proper implementation of the policy. Non-compliance will be her sole responsibility/accountability, being the head of the department.
observations.managementAction: AA Submitted the R policy on the procurement and disposition of medicines 2016-002 and is now being implemented.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the SWMP formalize a change management strategy in the implementation of a realistic and doable Solid Waste Management Plan. Develop an action/implementation Plan, draw a robust arrangement for the Plan And identify possible risks in a comprehensive manner for SWMB to facilitate proper implementation, monitoring and measurement of results, and to establish accountability for results.
observations.managementAction: AA Partially implemented. The revised 10 year plan covering 2017-2027 is still under preparation and is on-going as of audit report and still to be submitted to EMB-DENR.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the LCE should strengthen the coordination among Stakeholders by strictly adhering to the Memorandum of Agreement on the Grants from NGAs and the related laws and rules and regulations to ensure that the financial projects are properly implemented and monitored from its inception to its completion. For transparency purposes, there should be a tool to properly monitor the project from its inception to its completion.
observations.managementAction: AA The LCE should strengthen the coordination among Stakeholders by fully adhering to the Memorandum of Agreement on the Grants from NGAs; Only two BUBs this year and they have been strictly monitored from the Office of the LCE on the Status of the implementation. Moreover, for transparency purposes, there should be a tool to properly monitor the project from its inception to its completion.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the LCE should require the GSO to insure all Insurable Properties of the LGU in compliance to the Republic Act 656 and Proclamation No. 1081 dated September 21, 1972 and General Order No. 1, dated September 22, 1972. This includes inviting the GSIS personnel to assess the cost of insurance premium, and finally this will be included in their budget.
observations.managementAction: AA MVs were all insured but the Infra are still under inventory to finalize the list of insurable PPEs, and the LGU is still in the process of finalizing their list of PPE, and they will invite the GSIS personnel to assess the cost of insurance premium, and finally this will be included in their budget.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management require the concerned officials and employees to liquidate immediately the outstanding cash advances, and there should be no other than undeposited collections of outstanding cash advances which were included as cash advances to an appropriate account. Moreover, require the Accountant to reclassify the undeposited collections which were included as cash advances to an appropriate account.
observations.managementAction: AA Partially implemented. Cash advances other than undeposited collections were reclassified. Management require the concerned officials and employees to liquidate immediately the outstanding cash advances and the Accountant to reclassify the undeposited collections which were included as cash advances to an appropriate account.

observations.recommendation:
We recommend that the LCE should strengthen the coordination among
stakeholders by fully adhering to the Memorandum of Agreement on the
grants from NGAs and the related laws, rules and regulations to ensure that
the project are properly implemented. Moreover, for transparency purposes,
there should be a tool to properly monitor the project from its inception to its
completion.

We recommend that:
a. Ensure that the MSWDO and LGU is guided with the MOA on the
Roles and Responsibilities of the Parties in the proper implementation of the
project, this year;
b. Submit to this Commission a copy of the Status Report of the Project;
c. Moving forward, for future projects, ensure that these are properly
complied with in accordance with the MOA. Any deviation which is beyond
the control of the LGU should be properly reported to the concerned
authorities/stakeholders.

We recommend that the RHU Department (being part of the Management team) in collaboration with the Accounting Department to:
a. Immediately craft a well-defined guidelines through an internal
policy, on the Request for Procurement, Receipt, Disposition and
Monitoring of the Medicines and Medical supplies/equipment to ensure
that accountabilities and safeguarding, including risk ownership are in
place while in its custody.
b. The written guidelines may include, among others:
b.1.Request for Medicines:
1. Barangay Health Profiling should be done annually to all 42
barangays;
2. This in turn should be submitted to the Public Health Nurse (PHN)
for the preparation of the LGU Consolidated Report on Barangay
Health Profile;
3. The Consolidated Report on Barangay Health Profile should be
reconciled with the Medicines and Medical Supplies/equipment
Inventory Report
4. The result of the reconciliation will now serve as the basis for the
preparation for the request of stocks to avoid shortage of
medicines/medical supplies/equipment.
5. A Purchase Request should be prepared by the MHO and forwarded
to the LCE for the approval and eventual procurement.
b.2. Receipt of Medicines:
1. All receipt of medicines and medical supplies/equipment should be
thoroughly inspected by the Custodian and an RHU representative
simultaneously using the Delivery Receipt and Purchase Request as
their basis. Take note of the Quantity of Medicines and the Expiration
dates;
2. The Medicines received should be posted in the logbook inventory. A
separate set of logbook should be utilized for stocks from different
sources (LGU/ DOH/Province)
3. Ensure that these are properly stocked
4. In cases where the custodian is not available, there should be an
medicine custodian-alternate, designated by the MHO, and a proper
turn-over should be made.
5. There should be a registry for medicines for all receipts and issuances
to ensure proper tracking of the medicines and /medical
supplies/equipment
b.3. Issuances of Medicines
1. A copy of the medical receipts/request for release from the
RHU/Mayor should be evidenced every time there is an issuance of
medicines and medical supplies/equipment
2. It should be attached to the Authorized RHU Medicine slip and signed
by the custodian and recipient
3. It should be filed in a designated folder for Medicines Issue Slip for
easy reference.
4. The recipient should sign in the logbook upon release of the medicines
5. A monthly Report of issuances should be prepared and these should
be reconciled with the stocks available
b.4. Monitoring:
1. Conduct of Physical Inventory/ Inspection Report at least twice a
month
2. Post the List of Inventory in a conspicuous place of the RHU
The MHO, should strictly ensure the proper implementation of the
policy. Non-compliance will be her sole responsibility/accountability, being
the head of the department

We recommend that the LCE should require the GSO to insure all
Insurable Properties of the LGU the soonest possible time to ensure that the
LGU will be indemnified in case of losses due to manmade and catastrophic
events

We recommend that the SWMPB formalize a change management strategy in
the implementation of a realistic and doable Solid Waste Management Plan
with the application of the SMART strategy, Develop an
Action/Implementation Plan, Draw a robust governance arrangement for the
Plan and Identify possible risks in a comprehensive manner for SWMB to
facilitate proper implementation, monitoring and measurement of results, and
to establish accountability for results.
Require the Municipal Treasurer to deposit the Special Education Tax.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Treasurer will activate the LGU's SEF Account with LBP and SEF collections will be taken from the said account. Moreover, the newly appointed Budget Officer will prepare the SEF Annual Sanggunian Budget and the same will have to be approved by the Local School Board and finally be submitted to SB for legislation.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management require the concerned officials and employees to immediately liquidate their outstanding cash advances, and there should be no additional cash advance to any official or employee who is no longer connected with the LGU, and the outstanding cash advance given to him is first settled or a proper accounting thereof is made, as required by Section 89 of PD 1445. Moreover, require the Accountant to reclassify the undeposited collections which were included as cash advances to an appropriate account.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant will issue demand letters to the concerned officers and employees to immediately liquidate their outstanding cash advances, and will not allow additional cash advances to officers and employees with outstanding cash advances.
observations.reason: Demand letters were issued to these concerned officers and employees to immediately liquidate their outstanding cash advances and will not allow additional cash advances to these individuals.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the management prepare the Detailed Program Design for the Supplemental Feeding Program and ensure the procurement process for the required meals is in consonance with the MOA with DSWD.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Social Welfare and Development Officer will be directed to prepare the procurement process of the required meals for the Supplemental Feeding Program which is in consonance with the MOA with DSWD. Furthermore, she is instructed to strictly implement the MOA.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management exert extra efforts in the acquisition of lot for the establishment of the Nursery Farmers’ Center. If it would be impossible for them to acquire the said lot, then they should consider other development projects which are beneficial to the farmers and chargeable to the 20% Development Fund, and submit them to Sanggunian for realignment purposes.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Treasurer will resolve the lot issue where this project is to be constructed so that the construction can start. If it is impossible to acquire the said lot, consider other development projects chargeable to the 20% Development Fund and submit to Sanggunian for realignment.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management require the Accountant to submit immediately all copies of the Memorandum of Agreement for all fund transfers received to the Audit Team for verification purposes.
observations.managementAction: The LGU will submit the MOA to COA.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management submit said lacking supporting documents to COA. Henceforth, pay only transactions with complete supporting documents to avoid recurrence of this observation.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant will submit immediately all lacking supporting documents to COA. Henceforth, pay only transactions with complete supporting documents.

observations.recommendation: The Local Chief Executive should create an Inventory Committee that will focus on the conduct of the physical inventory of all properties of the LGU. The GSO shall prepare and update the Property Cards and Property Plant and Equipment Ledger Cards and submit the updated physical inventory report to the Accounting Department for reconciliation.
observations.managementAction: The LGU has already an Inventory Team that will conduct the complete physical count of all properties of the municipality. The GSO shall prepare and update the Property Cards and Property Ledger Cards. The Accounting Department shall prepare and update the Real Property Cards and Property Cards. The periodic physical inventory shall be strictly conducted onwards after the initial count in compliance with Section 124, Chapter 7, Volume 1 of the NGAs Manual for LGUs.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management strictly enforce NBC No. 486 and the ceiling of P1,200/day for seminars, trainings and conventions.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant will enforce the ceiling of P1,200/day for vouchers. However, since there is a new circular issued in April 2016 increasing the ceiling from P1,200.00 per day to P2,000.00 per day per NBC #563, the Accountant will enforce the new ceiling mandated.

observations.recommendation: Review the local tax code; create a system to enhance the collections of the local revenues; direct the Municipal Treasurer and Municipal Assessor to update their real property tax records against which they can set their collection targets; conduct frequent physical surveys to ensure that all real properties are properly listed, regularly appraised and actually paid on time.

It is recommended that the recorded valuation of the Land, Land Improvements and Buildings be traced to its historical cost and be reconciled with the real property records of the Municipal Assessor. Likewise, the parcels of land owned by the municipality should be supported either with an original or transfer certificate of title registered in the name of the LGU.
